steps.title: Meet the harness
steps.narration: This is the whole front door. One zero-dependency dispatcher on a home machine, and every readiness check, receipt, and benchmark in the flywheel starts right here.
steps.stdout:
usage: run_harness_cli.py [-h] [--python PYTHON] [--print-command]
                          {manifest,registry,benchmarks,forum-route,mcp-health,codex-mcp-contract,package-doctor,architecture-report,enterprise-readiness,tool-contract,runtime-contract,benchmark-coverage,comparison,execution-matrix,schematic-drift,agentic-tasks,cross-harness,adapter-runtime,embodied-realtime,model-card-claims,tool-hardening,classifier-friction,endpoint-gate,endpoint-launch-readiness,serve-launch,serve-resource,model-publish,plan,seed,outcome,query,readiness}
                          ...

Zero-dependency front controller for the local Codex/Flywheel harness.

positional arguments:
  {manifest,registry,benchmarks,forum-route,mcp-health,codex-mcp-contract,package-doctor,architecture-report,enterprise-readiness,tool-contract,runtime-contract,benchmark-coverage,comparison,execution-matrix,schematic-drift,agentic-tasks,cross-harness,adapter-runtime,embodied-realtime,model-card-claims,tool-hardening,classifier-friction,endpoint-gate,endpoint-launch-readiness,serve-launch,serve-resource,model-publish,plan,seed,outcome,query,readiness}
    manifest            emit executable surface manifest
    registry            emit static local HTML command registry
    benchmarks          emit weighted benchmark profile manifest
    forum-route         emit metadata-only Forum route receipts
    mcp-health          emit metadata-only MCP/tool health receipts
    codex-mcp-contract  emit Codex MCP launch and fallback contract
    package-doctor      emit package release doctor
    architecture-report
                        emit harness architecture and endpoint report
    enterprise-readiness
                        emit mneme/relay/plexus enterprise readiness report
    tool-contract       emit packaged sidecar tool integration contract
    runtime-contract    emit packaged runtime activation contract
    benchmark-coverage  compare benchmark profile against scorecards
    comparison          synthesize Codex-vs-Flywheel comparison report
    execution-matrix    emit benchmark execution matrix without running
                        benchmarks
    schematic-drift     check closed-loop schematic drift without running
                        benchmarks
    agentic-tasks       emit non-executing agentic task manifest
    cross-harness       emit non-executing cross-harness manifest
    adapter-runtime     emit metadata-only adapter/runtime compatibility
                        matrix
    embodied-realtime   emit non-executing embodied realtime multimodal
                        benchmark plan
    model-card-claims   emit non-executing model-card claim table
    tool-hardening      generate enterprise hardening plan from tool readiness
    classifier-friction
                        run guardrail/accountability friction benchmark
    endpoint-gate       run bounded local model endpoint health/generation
                        gate
    endpoint-launch-readiness
                        emit local model serve launch readiness and port-owner
                        diagnostics
    serve-launch        plan or start local harness/serve.py from endpoint
                        profiles
    serve-resource      emit GPU resource preflight before local serve launch
    model-publish       generate 14B/32B model naming and publish plan
    plan                emit closed-loop seed dry plan
    seed                execute the closed-loop seed run
    outcome             synthesize closed-loop outcome
    query               query the file-backed harness store
    readiness           run one metadata-only readiness receipt

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --python PYTHON
  --print-command       print the target command without executing it
steps.output:

steps.title: Release readiness receipt
steps.narration: Before anything ships, the harness writes itself a readiness receipt. This is the actual artifact from disk, not a slide. It says plainly what is ready and what still gates the release.
steps.stdout:
# Model release readiness receipt

- Schema: `harness.model-release-readiness/v1`
- Timestamp UTC: `2026-07-09T18:51:50.331379Z`
- Secret policy: metadata-only; model file bodies are not read; large model files are not hashed or copied
- Existing models: `2` / `2`
- Trained models: `1` / `2`
- Trained artifacts present: `1`
- Models with weights: `2`
- Release-ready static models: `1`
- Endpoint profile matches: `5`
- Endpoint gate rows: `2`
- Endpoint gate generation OK: `1`
- Benchmark artifact matches: `2`

| Model | Verdict | Trained | Root exists | Weights | Doc score | Endpoint profiles | Endpoint gate OK | Benchmark artifacts |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 14B | MODEL_RELEASE_READY_STATIC | true | true | 1 | 1.0 | 3 | 1 | 2 |
| 32B | MODEL_NO_TRAINED_ARTIFACT | false | true | 14 | 0.3846 | 2 | 0 | 0 |

steps.title: Endpoint gate receipt
steps.narration: The endpoint gate is the health check for the local model servers. Same idea: a small, honest file that records what was actually observed when the gate last ran.
steps.stdout:
# Model endpoint gate

- Schema: `harness.model-endpoint-gate/v1`
- Profiles selected: `2` / `5`
- Health OK rows: `2`
- Generation OK rows: `1`
- Failed rows: `1`

| Model | Backend | Role | Health | Status | Health ref | Generation | Failure | Latency ms |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---|---:|
| 14B | ollama | ollama_local | true | 200 |  | false | endpoint_error | 48.37 |
| 14B | ollama | ollama_local | true | 200 |  | true |  | 523.991 |
steps.output:

steps.title: Live local generation
steps.narration: Now the fun part. We ask the 14B coder living on this box to continue a function, no cloud involved. If the endpoint is asleep the step says SKIPPED honestly and the recording stays truthful.
steps.stdout:
endpoint: http://127.0.0.1:11434 (ollama 0.31.1)
model:    flywheel-local-coder-14b
prompt:   'def fibonacci(n):'  (num_predict=48, temperature=0)
generated in 64918 ms
--- completion ---
def fibonacci(n):Sure! Here's a simple implementation of the Fibonacci sequence in Python:

```python
def fibonacci(n):
    if n <= 0:
        return "Input should be a positive integer"
    elif n == 1:
        return 
--- end completion ---
tokens: 48 (0.7 tok/s)
